metadata.description: General non-negotiation assertiveness pairs for probing only. Tests whether the assertiveness vector generalises beyond negotiation contexts to everyday situations requiring assertiveness.
metadata.note: Contexts deliberately avoid negotiation, pricing, contracts, and business deals.

dimensions.name: Assertiveness
dimensions.description: Making direct, confident requests and statements without aggression; stating needs and positions clearly

pairs.context: A restaurant has brought you the wrong dish and when you mention it the waiter seems dismissive.
pairs.positive: I ordered the pasta, not the risotto. I'd like the correct dish please, and I'd appreciate it being prioritised since the rest of my table is already eating.
pairs.negative: It's fine, I can probably just eat around it. I don't want to make a fuss and slow everything down.

pairs.context: A friend keeps cancelling plans at short notice and has done so four times in a row.
pairs.positive: I want to be honest with you — this is the fourth time in a row you've cancelled last minute and it's making me feel like your time is more valuable than mine. I need this to change.
pairs.negative: It's really no problem, honestly. Life gets busy. We'll find a time that works eventually, I'm sure.

pairs.context: A doctor is dismissing your symptom concerns and moving to end the appointment.
pairs.positive: I'm not satisfied with where we've landed on this. I've had these symptoms for six weeks and I'd like them properly investigated before this appointment ends.
pairs.negative: Okay, I suppose you know best. Maybe I'm overthinking it. I'll just keep an eye on it and come back if it gets worse.

pairs.context: A neighbour has been playing loud music late at night on weeknights despite previous hints from you.
pairs.positive: I need to be direct with you — the music is too loud after 10pm on weeknights. I've hinted at this before but it hasn't changed. I need it to stop by 10pm from now on.
pairs.negative: I hate to bother you. The music is sometimes a bit loud but I understand it's your home. I just thought I'd mention it in case you hadn't realised.

pairs.context: You are being spoken to rudely by a customer service representative on the phone.
pairs.positive: I'd like you to speak to me politely. I haven't been rude to you and I expect the same in return. If that's not possible, I'd like to speak to a manager.
pairs.negative: I suppose they're probably having a bad day. I'll just stay patient and hope it improves.

pairs.context: A group project member has done no work and expects to share the credit for the final submission.
pairs.positive: I need to raise this before we submit. You haven't contributed to this project and I'm not comfortable with you sharing equal credit. We need to sort this out with the tutor.
pairs.negative: I don't want to cause drama in the group. I'll just finish the work and hopefully the lecturer won't ask too many questions about who did what.

pairs.context: A hairdresser has cut your hair much shorter than you asked and is seeking approval.
pairs.positive: This isn't what I asked for — I said to take off two centimetres and this is significantly shorter than that. I'm not happy with this.
pairs.negative: It's a bit shorter than I wanted, but it'll grow back. Thanks anyway, I'm sure it looks fine.

pairs.context: A family member repeatedly makes unwanted comments about your weight at family gatherings.
pairs.positive: I need to say this clearly: comments about my weight are unwelcome and I want them to stop. I've not brought it up before but I'm telling you directly now.
pairs.negative: It's awkward to say something, especially at a family gathering. I'll just try to brush it off and change the subject.

pairs.context: A colleague at work takes credit for your idea in a team meeting.
pairs.positive: I'd like to clarify something — that idea came from a conversation you and I had earlier where I outlined the approach. I'd like that to be noted for the record.
pairs.negative: It doesn't matter, really. The important thing is the idea gets used. I'll make sure to follow up directly with the manager later.

pairs.context: A friend asks you to do them a favour that would cause you significant inconvenience.
pairs.positive: I'm not able to do that. The timing doesn't work for me and it would cause me a real problem. I hope you understand.
pairs.negative: I suppose I could try to make it work somehow. I hate saying no, especially to a friend. Let me see if I can shuffle things around.

pairs.context: A hotel room has a maintenance problem that was reported at check-in but still hasn't been fixed by the next morning.
pairs.positive: This still hasn't been fixed. I reported it yesterday at check-in. I need it resolved today or I need to be moved to a different room. This isn't acceptable service.
pairs.negative: I mentioned it yesterday but it still seems to be an issue. I know you're all busy. I'll probably just manage for the rest of the stay.

pairs.context: A gym instructor is pushing you to do an exercise you have said is causing you pain.
pairs.positive: I've told you this is causing me pain and I'm not going to continue with it. If you want to suggest a modification that doesn't hurt, I'm open to that.
pairs.negative: I suppose they know what's best. I'll push through the pain and hope it settles down. I don't want to seem like I'm not trying.

pairs.context: Someone cuts in front of you in a long queue.
pairs.positive: Excuse me — I was here before you. The queue starts at the back.
pairs.negative: It's annoying but it's not worth making a scene about. I'll just let it go.

pairs.context: Your flatmate has been using your food without asking despite previous indirect hints.
pairs.positive: I need to say this directly: please don't use my food without asking. It's happened several times and my hints haven't worked, so I'm being explicit about it now.
pairs.negative: I keep labelling things but it doesn't seem to make a difference. I don't want to make things awkward at home so I'll just buy extra.

pairs.context: A mechanic quotes you a price that is significantly higher than the estimate they gave over the phone.
pairs.positive: You quoted me a different price over the phone. I have the message here. I'm not going to pay significantly more than what was agreed. Let's talk about what happened.
pairs.negative: It's more than I expected but these things always seem to go over estimate. I'll just pay it and not come back here.
